tasks.description: Daily 2am PT poker intel briefing for The Only Friends Podcast, posted to Discord #intel-staging
Daily 3am PT world news & trends briefing for The Only Friends Podcast, posted to Discord #intel-staging
Daily 4am PT memes & culture drop for The Only Friends Podcast, posted to Discord #intel-staging
Daily 7am PT run-of-show for The Only Friends Podcast. Posts a condensed single-page brief to #run-of-show and the full detailed ROS to #intel-staging.

Daily 8am PT thumbnail generator for The Only Friends Podcast — reads ROS from #run-of-show, produces A/B for biggest story + alternative for 2nd biggest, judge enforces ≥70 with rebuilds, posts 3 images to #thumbnails.

Daily 8:15am PT producer audit before 8:30 pre-production for The Only Friends Podcast. Audits #intel-staging / ROS / thumbnails, promotes assets + design requests out of #intel-staging, and acts on Matt's manual notes in #producer.
Primary 7:30am PT show-graphics sweep for The Only Friends Podcast — reads #graphic-design requests, generates tone-driven 1280×720 graphics via Nano Banana Pro, posts to #assets
Late 8:05am PT show-graphics sweep for The Only Friends Podcast — catches late adds and any graphics flagged by thumbnails, finishes before 8:15 producer audit
Daily 5am PT viral-hand research cron for The Only Friends Podcast. Summary of ranked hands → #intel-staging; clip link → #assets. Feeds the B block.
Writes /Users/mjb11/Documents/Claude/Projects/Media Company Infrastructure/web/data.json every 5 minutes so the Mission Control static site has fresh data.
tasks.schedule: Every 5 minutes, every hour, every day

1489392874029187304.content: berkey11: spawn the designer with 4 Nano Banana comps of the OF badge — we're going for a sophisticated look. Can subtly include generic imagery like suits and a mic, just do it abstractly. Lock in the color palette, just be sure to include more than just blue, white or negative space is fine.

**Graphic Designer:**

Now converting the JPGs to PNGs and dropping them in the outbox.All four PNGs are in the outbox. Here's what was built:

---

**OF Badge — 4 Concept Comps**
*(All generated via Nano Banana Pro / gemini-3-pro-image-preview)*

**Comp A — Charcoal Gold**
Deep charcoal black base, "OF" serif monogram in champagne gold, burnished bronze hairline circle border with subtle herringbone textile texture suggesting tailored fabric. Warmest, most classic of the four — luxury whiskey label energy.

**Comp B — Burgundy Navy**
Oxblood burgundy meets midnight navy split-field within the badge circle. Abstract suit geometry in negative space at center, waveform dot arc for the mic suggestion, ivory and rose-gold accents. Most dramatic tonal contrast — poker room at 2am.

**Comp C — Cream Forest Brass**
Inverted palette — warm cream/off-white field, forest green and aged brass as accent tones. The most editorial and Swiss-influenced. Unique in the set; would stand out on a light-background feed without losing sophistication.

**Comp D — Plum Platinum**
Near-black plum base with violet undertones, concentric platinum rings suggesting a mic grille from above, pinstripe radiating texture at low opacity, warm amber + steel-blue tonal contrast. Most cinematic/nocturnal — high-end spirits label or luxury wax seal feel.

Let me know which direction resonates (or if you want to mash-up elements from multiple comps) and I'll push it to vector.
berkey11: ok, rerun it but stay true to our blue color scheme. Additionally be a little less abstract as these just look like random geometric figures

**Graphic Designer:**

All four generated. Now converting to PNGs and dropping them in the outbox.All 4 PNGs are in the outbox. Here's what was built — all generated via Nano Banana Pro (gemini-3-pro-image-preview):

---

**Comp A — Navy + Champagne Gold**
Deep navy `#003366` base. Suit jacket silhouette centered, lapels and sleeves clearly readable. Bold serif "OF" monogram overlaid in champagne gold. Gold circular border ring, subtle embossed texture. The most classic/heraldic of the four — luxury members club energy.

**Comp B — Royal Blue + Chrome Silver**
Vivid royal blue with a clearly recognizable upright condenser microphone at center — grille detail, stand, the works. "OF" in bright white with silver shadow above the mic. Sound wave arcs in silver flanking. Double-ring chrome border. Audio brand meets premium broadcast aesthetic.

**Comp C — Midnight Blue + Warm Gold Filigree**
The most detailed comp — suited figure and condenser mic together in gold line-art, "OF" arced at the top, "ONLY FRIENDS" in small gold spaced caps along the bottom arc. Ornate gold filigree border. Deep midnight `#001845`. Heraldic, almost like a wax seal or whiskey label.

**Comp D — Cerulean/Steel Gradient + Platinum Silver**
Most contemporary of the four. Large bold "OF" in white metallic takes foreground, suit-and-tie silhouette behind in subtle darker blue, small mic icon below. Polished steel silver beveled border. Cerulean-to-steel gradient gives it depth without going dark. Premium podcast/media brand feel.

Let me know which direction(s) to push to vector in Illustrator — I can mash up elements from multiple comps or dial in a specific one.
berkey11: I don't really like the suit concept. I do like the idea of a subtle image in the background though.

Got it. Before I rerun these, what background imagery direction appeals to you? A few options:

- **Poker table / cards** — subtle card edge or felt texture visible behind the badge
- **Stage / spotlight** — lighting rig or stage floor implied
- **Mic + stand only** — ditch the suit entirely, keep just the microphone as the readable foreground element
- **Texture / pattern** — herringbone weave, pinstripe, or geometric pattern that suggests luxury/sophistication without depicting anything literal
- **Crowd silhouette** — blurred figures in background suggesting an audience

Or something else entirely? Just want to nail the vibe before I regenerate.
berkey11: Yeah maybe we go crowd silhouette and then work in abstract card suit(s) and/or mic

**Graphic Designer:**

All 4 PNGs are in the outbox. Here's the breakdown — all generated via Nano Banana Pro (gemini-3-pro-image-preview):

---

**Comp A — Navy + Champagne Gold, Suit Symbols**
Deep navy badge, bold serif "OF" in champagne gold, abstract spade/diamond accents at cardinal positions, blurred poker room crowd barely visible in the background. Most classic/heraldic of the four.

**Comp B — Royal Blue + Silver, Mic Forward**
Royal-to-steel blue gradient, "OF" in silver-white metallic, abstract condenser mic in silver line art beneath the monogram, heart/club suit symbols at the badge edges. Most broadcast/audio-forward.

**Comp C — Midnight Blue + Gold Filigree, Full Suite**
Deepest, most ornate — all four suit symbols in gold around the "OF", "ONLY FRIENDS" arced along the bottom, minimal mic icon below the monogram, filigree border ring. The most detailed and wax-seal/whiskey-label in feel.

**Comp D — Cerulean/Navy Gradient + Platinum, Suits + Mic**
Most contemporary — cerulean-to-navy radial gradient, platinum "OF", spade/diamond in fine platinum line work, slim mic silhouette at base. Clean hierarchy, premium without being heavy.

Let Matt know which direction (or element mix) to push to Illustrator and I'll build the vector.
berkey11: Call the graphic designer and feed him these specific adjustments

**Graphic Designer:**

Now converting to PNG and dropping in the outbox:All 4 PNGs delivered to the outbox. Here's the breakdown — all via Nano Banana Pro (gemini-3-pro-image-preview):

---

**Comp A — Navy + Poker Felt**
Deep navy base. Gold condenser mic as the clear hero. Behind it: dark green/blue poker felt texture at low opacity — just enough to nod to the poker world without screaming it. Champagne gold "OF" serif + bottom arc lettering. Classic members club energy.

**Comp B — Midnight Blue + Stage Spotlight**
Midnight-to-royal blue gradient. Chrome/silver vintage broadcast mic centered. Subtle warm amber spotlight cone hitting a stage floor behind it — cinematic, live-show atmosphere. "OF" in white + gold arc. The most cinematic of the four.

**Comp C — Cerulean + Herringbone Texture**
Cerulean blue base. Modern gold condenser mic, prominent. Background: ultra-subtle herringbone weave pattern in slightly lighter blue — luxury fabric feel, zero literalism. Platinum ring border. The most editorial/refined — best for merch, apparel tags, print applications.

**Comp D — Midnight Blue + Crowd Silhouette**
Near-black midnight base. Gold mic in the foreground. Behind it: softly blurred audience silhouettes — suggests a live poker show or broadcast event, deeply atmospheric. Ornate gold ring border. The most dramatic/cinematic of the set.

---

**Designer's pick:** Comp B or D for digital/social (most immediate visual impact), Comp C for any physical/merch application (texture reads beautifully in print). Let me know which direction to push to Illustrator for the final vector build.

**B BLOCK:** WSOP Rings Debut in Texas Tomorrow (While The Lodge Sits Frozen) | USPO Championship Tonight
**C BLOCK:** Zobian's USPO #9 + Children's Nonprofit / Hellmuth Home Game Ep 20 "Quads" Moment / Wemby Concussion Tanks Spurs Futures / Cartillier's Cinderella WSOPC Win

📺 **Top YT title:** "Stephen Chidwick Just Admitted He's Only Kept $10M of His $76M in Lifetime Poker Cashes"
🎬 **Best clip:** A Block ~0:14:00–0:17:00 — Matusow's "mirage" quote + Chidwick's $5–10M / $76M reply + Matt's "what did you keep" line

*[cowork] Run-of-Show Agent · April 22, 2026 · 7:25 AM PT*

---

**C BLOCK continued — Items 3 & 4** — Wemby fall / Spurs futures / OKC favorite; Cartillier 3rd cash = gold ring (cut first). No memes drop today.
**🔚 CLOSE** + Algorithm notes: best clip, 3 YT title options, trending angle (money-reveal)
